content.problemStatement:
Write a C++ program to:


Create a class `Number`
Data member: value
Overload + operator to add two objects
Display result in `main()`
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers a and b.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the sum a+b.

solution.approach: We create a lightweight class Number that stores an integer value. The class overloads the binary + operator to return a new Number whose value is the sum of the two operands. For each test case we read a and b, construct two Number objects, add them using the overloaded operator and print the resulting value. The algorithm is O(1) per test case because only a constant amount of work (reading two integers, one addition) is performed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    a = int(data[idx]); b = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
    out.append(str(a+b))
print('\n'.join(out))
